100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 12, 1926, a significant political shift occurred as the British government faced mounting pressure over its decision to continue negotiations with the Irish Free State. Meanwhile, in Germany, tensions rose as the Weimar Republic grappled with economic instability and rising unemployment. In the United States, the Prohibition era continued to spark debates, with calls for reform growing louder amid widespread illegal activities.
